title: Multiply Using Partial Products
essential_question: How do partial products help you multiply?

pretest.question: In partial products for 4 × 23, one partial product is:

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Work through it step by step.

hints: Multiplication is repeated addition. Use known facts when possible.

pretest.question: Partial products of 6 × 54: 6×50 + 6×4 = 300 + ___ = ___
hints: Think about what partial products means.
Try drawing an array or using place value.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 24. Multiply place by place.

pretest.question: 7 × 48 partial products: 7×40=280, 7×8=56, sum = ___
hints: Think about what partial products means.
Try drawing an array or using place value.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 336. Multiply place by place.

pretest.question: Partial products and Distributive Property produce the same answer.

feedback.incorrect: The answer is True.

hints: Distributive splits one factor: a × (b + c) = a×b + a×c.

pretest.question: 8 × 256 partial products sum to ___
hints: Try drawing an array or using place value.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is 2048. Multiply place by place.

learn.title: What Are Partial Products?
learn.content: Partial products are the small products you get when you multiply by each place value of the other factor. Add them to get the total.
learn.tts: Partial products are the small products you get when you multiply by each place value of the other factor. Add them to get the total.

learn.title: Set Up a Grid
learn.content: Draw a rectangle split by place value. Label sides with the digits. Multiply within each cell.
learn.tts: Draw a rectangle split by place value. Label sides with the digits. Multiply within each cell.

learn.title: Vertical Algorithm
learn.content: Write the partial products in a column, one per row, aligned by place value. Add them up.
learn.tts: Write the partial products in a column, one per row, aligned by place value. Add them up.

learn.title: No Regrouping Needed
learn.content: Partial products don't require regrouping during multiplication — you keep each product whole until the final addition.
learn.tts: Partial products don't require regrouping during multiplication — you keep each product whole until the final addition.

learn.title: Why It Helps
learn.content: Partial products makes every step visible, which prevents errors and helps you spot mistakes.
learn.tts: Partial products makes every step visible, which prevents errors and helps you spot mistakes.
